mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tight labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which validates higher equity risk premiums and sustains positive S&P 500 momentum over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any consecutive 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate leverage and equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where the rate crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity prices over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging outflows and anticipates aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered months over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and consumer credit balances confirms robust household balance sheet expansion, which fuels discretionary spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in industrial production greater than 1.0 percent signals manufacturing sector contraction, which reduces corporate order books and forces negative S&P 500 adjustments over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where the 3-month INDPRO change falls below -1.0%.
